events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection result directly to `/app/answer.json` using the scenario’s timing-mark cue and OEM tolerance expectation.

/bin/sh -lc "cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 70,
  \"value\": 1,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.92,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Timing marks are misaligned by approximately one tooth.\",
    \"Valve timing is outside OEM alignment tolerance for assembly closeout.\",
    \"Condition risks poor running, no-start, misfire, or valve-to-piston contact on interference engines.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not rotate or run the engine as accepted work.\",
    \"Remove tension as required by OEM procedure and reset cam/crank timing marks to exact alignment.\",
    \"Re-tension timing drive per OEM service information and recheck marks through manual rotation before closeout.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The visible timing reference marks are off by one tooth. OEM timing procedures require the camshaft and crankshaft marks to align exactly at the specified indexing position; a one-tooth error changes valve timing enough to be out of tolerance and cannot pass a quality gate at rough-complete.\",
  \"references\": [\"OEM service info - timing drive alignment marks and tensioning procedure\"]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"
